claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 14 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by accessing the official assessment framework.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the assessment. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to start with some basic information about your child.

**First question: How old is your child?**

The age is important because:
- Children **under 16** may automatically qualify
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have two children I'd like to ask about. My eldest, Blake, is 14, and my younger one, Alex, is 8. They both live at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess eligibility for both Blake (14) and Alex (8).

Since both of your children are under 16, they fall into the automatic qualification age category. However, I need to check a few more requirements to confirm eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - both Blake (14) and Alex (8) are under 16, which is the first step toward eligibility.

**Next question: Are either Blake or Alex working 24 hours or more per week in paid employment?**

(This is typically not common for children their age, but I need to confirm they're not working full-time hours or receiving certain benefits that would make them ineligible.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all. Blake's still in school and Alex is only 8, so they're both just focused on their studies.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check another requirement:

**Are either Blake or Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

(Again, this is uncommon for children their age, but I need to verify this to continue the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receive any benefits in their own name. They're just regular kids living at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue through the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Continuing on:

**Are either Blake (14) or Alex (8) enrolled in any type of apprenticeship program?**

(Since they're 14 and 8, this is unlikely, but I need to confirm they're not in any apprenticeship schemes that could affect eligibility.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are doing apprenticeships. They're both just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now we need to check your residency status. You mentioned you live in Manchester, which is great.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

If yes, that satisfies the residency requirement. 

(There are also other ways to meet this requirement, such as having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, being a Crown servant abroad, or other special circumstances - but if you're simply living in the UK normally, that's the most straightforward path.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live here normally. We're based in Manchester and have been for years.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That satisfies the residency requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for the children.

**You mentioned that both Blake and Alex live at home with you in Manchester. Is that correct?**

Living with the children is one of the key ways to demonstrate responsibility for them. (Other ways include contributing to their upkeep or paying maintenance, but living with them is the most straightforward.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Both Blake and Alex live at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That establishes your responsibility for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check an important requirement:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake or Alex?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- An ex-partner
- A relative

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. If someone else is claiming, you would need to agree on who should receive it, and the other person would need to stop their claim.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to check the living arrangements for Blake and Alex:

**Are either Blake or Alex currently:**
- In hospital for a long-term stay?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or are they living normally at home with you?

You mentioned they live at home with you, so I just need to confirm there are no special circumstances like hospital stays or care arrangements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both just living normally at home with me. No hospital stays, no care arrangements, nothing like that. Just regular home life.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Almost there! Just one final check:

**Is the local council paying towards Blake's or Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

(This would be things like the council paying for their housing or living expenses. Since they live at home with you and you're supporting them, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm this final requirement.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards them. I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Excellent News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**For both Blake (14) and Alex (8), you meet all the eligibility requirements.**

### Why You're Eligible:

✅ **Blake (14):**
- Under 16 years old (automatic qualification)
- Not working full-time or receiving benefits in their own right
- Not in an apprenticeship
- Lives with you and you're responsible for their care
- You meet UK residency requirements
- No one else is claiming for Blake
- No local council support involved

✅ **Alex (8):**
- Under 16 years old (automatic qualification)
- Not working full-time or receiving benefits in their own right
- Not in an apprenticeship
- Lives with you and you're responsible for their care
- You meet UK residency requirements
- No one else is claiming for Alex
- No local council support involved

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates) for Blake and Alex
4. **Be aware:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply (though you can still claim and choose not to receive payments if you want to protect your National Insurance credits)
5. **Payments:** Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Apply here:
👉 **https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim**

Do you have any questions about the application process or the High Income Child Benefit Charge?

reasonings: Blake (age 14) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them; (6) Claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally resident in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Alex (age 8) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them; (6) Claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally resident in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Blake (14) and Alex (8). Both children are under 16 and living at home with the claimant in Manchester. The claimant is responsible for both children, meets UK residency requirements, and no disqualifying factors apply to either child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care costs.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Blake (age 14) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them; (6) Claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally resident in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Alex (age 8) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them; (6) Claimant meets UK residency requirements (normally resident in Manchester); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Blake (14) and Alex (8). Both children are under 16 and living at home with the claimant in Manchester. The claimant is responsible for both children, meets UK residency requirements, and no disqualifying factors apply to either child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care costs.
